title: Prozzen Fosky

'''Prozzen Fosky''', also known as '''Prozzen "Mozzy" Fosky''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[jazz]] [[Musician/Legends|musician]] who lived on the Bith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]]. Despite the fact that he was a legendary [[mozz box]] performer to [[Music/Legends|music]] aficionados in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], Fosky was practically unknown to the public. While going between various jobs, Fosky frequently became embroiled in shady dealings and unknowingly used his [[Force-sensitive/Legends|connection]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to influence people to help him out of dangerous situations where he could have been [[Death/Legends|killed]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] named Prozzen Fosky lived on the Bith [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Clak'dor VII/Legends|Clak'dor VII]]. He was a skilled [[mozz box]] performer who played [[jazz]] [[Music/Legends|music]] and was sometimes called Prozzen "Mozzy" Fosky. Although he was extremely well-liked by music connoisseurs in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] and [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], Fosky was largely unknown by the general public. Due to his position, Fosky was frequently without [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to spend.<ref name="GATORW" />

During his career, he played in situations ranging from a few people in a [[Tapcafe/Legends|tapcafe]] to private audiences and even large music halls. In between his jobs as a [[Musician/Legends|musician]], Fosky was a participant in many marginally illegal activities where his life was put in danger. Due to his unconscious use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to influence people and his large number of friends, Fosky was able to avoid being [[Death/Legends|killed]] during times of danger.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Personality and traits==
Prozzen Fosky was a highly skilled mozz box performer, and was able to perform for both small informal gatherings and at large audience halls. He was fluent and literate in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], the [[Bith (language)/Legends|Bith]] [[Language/Legends|language]], and [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], while he was also able to speak [[Bothese]], [[Durese/Legends|Durese]], the [[Pak Pak/Legends|Neimoidian language]], [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]], the [[Twi'leki/Legends|Twi'lek language]], and the [[Zabraki/Legends|Zabrak language]]. While he was often mixed up in illegal activities and was nearly always short on funds, the main things that Fosky thought of as important were the stories he told and the music he played for other people.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Skills and abilities==
Although he did not know that he was Force-sensitive, Fosky still was able to unconsciously use the Force to influence others into helping him out of his problems. In addition to his [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]], Fosky was also adept in deceiving and persuading others in conventional ways. He was additionally skilled in using [[Computer/Legends|computers]], and unlike most Biths, Fosky was not a pacifist and was marginally skilled in using [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]].<ref name="GATORW" />

==Equipment==
Prozzen Fosky owned a high quality mozz box for his performances. He also had a [[Sporting blaster/Legends|sporting blaster]] pistol for personal defense.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Prozzen Fosky is featured in ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', a [[2004]] sourcebook written by [[Craig Robert Carey|Craig R. Carey]], [[Jason Fry]], [[Jeff Quick]], and [[Daniel Wallace]], for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook|revised edition]] of the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. Fosky is featured in the book as a non-player character in the section of the book about Clak'dor VII. Due to the fact that Fosky is intended to be usable as a non-player character in any era, it is impossible to determine in which [[List of publishing eras|timeframe]] Fosky lived.<ref name="GATORW" />

==Sources==
